_comment: Generic MCP server config for browser-bridge. Most clients (Claude Code's .mcp.json, Claude Desktop, and others) use this `mcpServers` shape — copy the `browser-bridge` entry into your client's config. Point `command` at the installed binary; install.sh puts it at ~/.browser-bridge/browser-bridge. Use an ABSOLUTE path (most clients do not expand ~), and it does not depend on your shell PATH. The server speaks MCP over stdio with no arguments. Codex uses TOML instead of JSON — see the README for that snippet.
